steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes science fiction's role in relation to 'real-world technological advancements.' The examples — Verne inspiring aerospace engineering, Star Trek sparking smartphone design — show science fiction actively driving those innovations. The blank needs a word meaning 'active cause or driver.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'diversion from': A diversion pulls attention away from something; science fiction here moves things toward innovation. ✗
  • Choice B — 'catalyst of': A catalyst triggers or accelerates a process. The passage supports this with 'inspiring,' 'sparking,' and 'spurred.' ✓
  • Choice C — 'constraint to': A constraint limits or blocks; the opposite of what the passage describes. ✗
  • Choice D — 'sponsor of': A sponsor provides funding or backing — passive financial support, not active creative inspiration. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
